# Schema: Spec Bundle
<!-- Defines the structure of the five files generated by /pm-ff. -->
<!-- Used as a reference by commands and by contributors building new integrations. -->

The spec bundle is written to `features/[feature-name]/` and consists of five Markdown files. `agent-brief.md` is the primary output — generated first, optimized for AI coding agents.

---

## agent-brief.md

**Purpose:** Structured task brief for AI coding agents. Primary output of `/pm-ff`. Fill in Technical Context before handing to an agent.

**Required fields:**
| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| Task summary | String | 1–2 sentences. Precise outcome, not implementation approach. |
| Product context | Object | Product description (1 sentence), affected user segment |
| Technical context | Object | Repo, relevant files, tech stack, related systems — PM fills in before use |
| In scope | List | Specific list of what to build |
| Out of scope | List | Explicit list — at least 2 items |
| Acceptance criteria | Checklist | Each item observable and testable |
| Edge cases | Table | Scenario → Expected behavior |
| Definition of done | Checklist | Tests, no regressions, analytics |
| Stop and ask if | List | Ambiguous decisions the agent should escalate, not guess |

**Quality check before handing to agent:**
- [ ] Technical Context section filled in (repo, files, stack)
- [ ] No acceptance criterion uses "looks good" or "works correctly" — must be observable
- [ ] Out of scope lists the 2 most tempting things to accidentally change
- [ ] "Stop and ask if" covers the top decision points the agent would otherwise guess at

---

## brief.md

**Purpose:** One-page summary for the PM and immediate team. Readable in 2 minutes.

**Required fields:**
| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| Feature name | String | Title-cased |
| Date | ISO date | YYYY-MM-DD |
| Author | String | PM name |
| Status | Enum | `Draft` \| `Review` \| `Approved` |
| Problem | String | 2–3 sentences. Who, what, why now. |
| Solution | String | 2–3 sentences. What we're building. |
| Success Metrics | Table | Metric, Baseline, Target, Timeframe |
| In scope | List | Bulleted |
| Out of scope | List | Bulleted — explicit exclusions |
| Key risks | List | 2–3 items |

**Max length:** 1 page (300–400 words)

---

## prd.md

**Purpose:** Complete requirements document for the team. Source of truth for what ships.

**Required fields:**
| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| Feature name | String | |
| Date | ISO date | |
| Status | Enum | `Draft` \| `Review` \| `Approved` |
| PM | String | |
| Stakeholders | List | |
| Background | Paragraph | Context — why now |
| Problem statement | String | 1–2 sentences. Crisp and falsifiable. |
| Goals | List | Tied to measurable outcomes |
| Non-goals | List | Explicit exclusions |
| User stories | List | Ordered by P0/P1/P2 priority |
| Requirements (P0) | List | Must-have |
| Requirements (P1) | List | Should-have |
| Requirements (P2) | List | Nice-to-have |
| Edge cases | Table | Scenario → Expected behavior |
| Open questions | Table | Question, Owner, Due |
| Success criteria | String | Observable, measurable |
| Dependencies | Table | Dependency, Team, Status, Needed by |
| Out of scope | Table | Item, Rationale |

**Max length:** No hard limit. Aim for completeness over brevity.

---

## approach.md

**Purpose:** Documents the decision-making behind the chosen implementation direction.

**Required fields:**
| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| Summary | Paragraph | Chosen approach + key reason |
| Options considered | List | Min 2 options. Each with pros/cons/why rejected. |
| Chosen option | Object | Include explicit "why chosen" |
| Technical considerations | Text | Known constraints. Flag unknowns. |
| Design considerations | Text | Key UX decisions |
| Rollout strategy | Object | Type, phase 1 audience, advance criteria, killswitch |
| Assumptions | List | What needs to be true for this to work |

---

## plan.md

**Purpose:** Milestones, work breakdown, and communication plan.

**Required fields:**
| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| Timeline | Table | Milestone, Description, Owner, Target date |
| Work breakdown | Checklist | By function: Discovery, Design, Eng, QA, Launch |
| Dependencies | Table | What, Team, Status, Needed by |
| Risks | Table | Risk, Likelihood, Impact, Mitigation |
| Communication plan | Table | Update, Audience, Channel, Date, Owner |

---

## Validation Rules

A spec bundle is considered complete when:
- [ ] All required fields in brief.md are filled (no blank required fields)
- [ ] Problem statement in prd.md is a single falsifiable statement (not a direction or solution)
- [ ] At least 2 user stories present, each with a specific user segment (not "users")
- [ ] Out-of-scope section has at least 1 explicit exclusion
- [ ] Success metrics have a baseline and a target (not just a direction)
- [ ] Approach.md has at least 2 options considered
- [ ] Plan.md has a target launch date
